language_rules: 'use crate_name::' and 'extern crate crate_name' count as usage

Crate name normalization:
  Cargo.toml uses hyphens: my-crate
  Rust source uses underscores: use my_crate::
  → normalize hyphens to underscores when comparing

Procedural macro crates are used via attribute syntax, NOT 'use' statements:
  #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)] → serde (with features=["derive"]) or serde_derive
  #[derive(Debug, Clone, ...)] → built-in, ignore
  Custom #[proc_macro_attribute] or #[derive(CustomTrait)] → check which crate provides it
build-dependencies: only check build.rs for usage; never flag as unused if build.rs exists and uses the crate
dev-dependencies: only check #[cfg(test)] blocks, tests/ directory, examples/ directory, benches/ directory

Optional dependencies (optional = true in Cargo.toml):
  These are gated behind feature flags — they are 'used' if the feature is declared in [features]
  Do NOT flag optional deps as unused without checking the [features] table
Conditional platform deps [target.'cfg(...)'.dependencies]: treat as used (platform-specific, conservative)
Workspace Cargo.toml: if [workspace] is present, member crates may use deps declared at workspace level — be conservative
Feature flags are not separate packages: serde/features=["derive"] is still just serde
Re-exported items: if crate A re-exports crate B's types publicly, B may appear nowhere in source but is still needed
missing_dep_rule: Normalize hyphens(-) to underscores(_) for comparison; check both forms
